Petition For Approval Of Interim Rept. Of Practice Acmin. Of The Law Practice Of Raymond H. Levy, Inc
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Byrne (Petitioner/Practice Administrator) in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that it received the declaration from Shirley Levy yesterday. Mr. Byrne stated that he had not seen the declaration and that he was at a little bit of a loss as to understanding the various concerns of the Court. Mr. Byrne also stated that the reason Mr. Levy set this up was so that someone could take over his practice and take care of his family. In addition, Mr. Byrne spoke of his working relationship with Shirley Levy, stated that he resolved many of Mr. Levy's existing cases, that the statute of limitations has run and that there is no opportunity for any client of third party to sue. The Petition for Approval of Interim Report of Practice Administration, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Interim Rept. Of Practice Acmin. Of The Law Practice Of Raymond H. Levy, Inc
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Continued To Oct-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Interim Rept. Of Practice Acmin. Of The Law Practice Of Raymond H. Levy, Inc
Off Calendar Sep-15-2014 Continued To Oct-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Interim Rept. Of Practice Acmin. Of The Law Practice Of Raymond H. Levy, Inc
Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Continued To Sep-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Byrne in Propria Persona.

Mr. Byrne stated that at the time Mr. Levy passed away he had moved all of his office equipment to his home and rented a desk at Mr. O'Keeffe's office, that they have nothing to appraise and that the only value to the Estate is the ongoing business. Mr. Byrne also explained that Mr. O'Keeffe also passed away, that he was asked by both families to come into the case, that the appraised value of $525,000.00 by the Probate Referee was a projected amount, that he (Mr. Byrne) estimated that he will pay about $500,000.00 for the next five years, that this was an oral contract and that he will continue to abide by the fee splitting contract. In addition, Mr. Byrne stated that he went out and collected $113,442.00 in accounts receivable, that the Trustee fees are at 25% and his hourly rate is at 10%, addressed the issue of conflict of interest and argued that Rule 3-310 does not apply.
The Petition for Approval of Interim Report of Practice Administrator was continued to September 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file any revised accounting. If satisfactory to the Court, the matter will be approved. If not, the matter will be continued to October 6, 2014.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Interim Rept. Of Practice Acmin. Of The Law Practice Of Raymond H. Levy, Inc
Off Calendar Aug-11-2014 Continued To Aug-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Byrne (Practice Administrator) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Byrnes explained that he used to work for Mr. Levy, that Gregory O'Keeffe was to take over Mr. Levy's practice, that Mr. O'Keeffe passed away and that the Family asked him to take over and wrap things up. Mr. Byrnes also stated that some of the Clients have asked him for representation, that no Client's interest is in conflict and there is no dual representation of any Client, that he is acting as Successor Counsel under Rule 3-310 and that there is a benefit to the estate. The Court stated that it is some what disturbing that Mr. Byrne is the practice administrator and the practice purchaser, and that there should be two hats not one. In addition, Mr. Byrne stated that the Estate has it's own Counsel, that he is not involved in the Estate matter at all, that all cases are resolved, that account receivables have been collected, that everything is already done, that his role has been known to the Court for the last 18-months, that the Court appointed him as Practice Administrator, that he did not ask for the position and that there is no detriment to anyone. The Petition for Approval of Interim Report was continued to August 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
